title: Highly Available RAG Platform with OPA, ETCD, and AI Gateway
code:
graph TD subgraph "End Users" CMS_USER[Platform Admin] RAG_USER[RAG Platform User] end subgraph "Network Layer" LB["Load Balancer (HA Pair)
e.g., HAProxy with Keepalived"] end subgraph "Datacenter 1" subgraph "App Node 1 (VM)" APISIX1["APISIX Gateway
(with AI Gateway plugins)"] CMS1[CMS Microservice] OPA1[OPA Microservice] RAG_APP1[RAG Application Service] end subgraph "Data Node 1 (VM)" ETCD1[(etcd Node 1)] VECTOR_DB1[(Vector Database)] end end subgraph "Datacenter 2" subgraph "App Node 2 (VM)" APISIX2["APISIX Gateway
(with AI Gateway plugins)"] CMS2[CMS Microservice] OPA2[OPA Microservice] RAG_APP2[RAG Application Service] end subgraph "Data Node 2 (VM)" ETCD2[(etcd Node 2)] VECTOR_DB2[(Vector Database)] end end subgraph "External AI Services" LLM["Large Language Models
(OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)"] end %% Connections CMS_USER --> LB RAG_USER --> LB LB --> APISIX1 & APISIX2 %% CMS Flow APISIX1 --> CMS1 & OPA1 CMS1 & OPA1 --> ETCD1 APISIX2 --> CMS2 & OPA2 CMS2 & OPA2 --> ETCD2 %% RAG App Flow APISIX1 --> RAG_APP1 RAG_APP1 --> VECTOR_DB1 APISIX2 --> RAG_APP2 RAG_APP2 --> VECTOR_DB2 %% LLM Request Flow (The Key Part) RAG_APP1 --> APISIX1 RAG_APP2 --> APISIX2 APISIX1 -.->|Proxies to| LLM APISIX2 -.->|Proxies to| LLM %% Data Replication ETCD1 <-.->|Replication| ETCD2 VECTOR_DB1 <-.->|Replication| VECTOR_DB2

ai.summary: A robust, highly available RAG platform architecture with configuration management, OPA for policy enforcement, ETCD for distributed config, and APISIX as
ai.whatItShows: This diagram illustrates a highly available Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) platform architecture. It features a Configuration Management Service (CMS), Open Policy Agent (OPA) for policy enforcement, and ETCD for distributed configuration, all deployed across two distinct datacenters. The architecture includes Load Balancers, APISIX as an API and AI Gateway, RAG Application Services, and Vector Databases, interacting with external Large Language Models (LLMs). It highlights data replication between datacenters for ETCD and the Vector Database, ensuring resilience and scalability.
ai.whenToUse: Use this architecture when designing a production-grade RAG platform that requires high availability, fault tolerance, centralized configuration management, and robust policy enforcement. It's suitable for applications needing to integrate with various LLMs securely and efficiently, especially in environments demanding multi-datacenter deployment for disaster recovery and geographic distribution.
ai.howToAdapt: This architecture can be adapted by integrating different Load Balancers (e.g., Nginx, AWS ELB), swapping the Vector Database, or connecting to alternative LLM providers. The APISIX AI Gateway can be extended with custom plugins for advanced AI-specific functionalities. Scaling can be achieved by adding more App and Data Nodes within or across datacenters. Policy rules in OPA and configurations in ETCD can be customized to fit specific application requirements and security policies.
